mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals an abrupt labor market deterioration that triggers immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction during documented labor market shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth that sustains corporate revenue expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects constrained supply-side capacity that compresses profit margins, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth during tightening periods produces positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of observations, or the predictive coefficient flips sign outside the tightening regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate wage income and consumer spending capacity, generating immediate positive momentum in equity markets over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive PAYEMS growth correlates with negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of monthly observations, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPI inflation above 3.0% triggers expectations of aggressive monetary policy normalization that increases discount rates and compresses equity multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of high-inflation periods, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions with p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly personal consumption expenditures signals a structural demand mismatch that erodes corporate earnings forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between GDPC1 and RSAFS shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, or positive returns dominate when divergence exceeds 1.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects persistent supply chain bottlenecks that delay earnings rebounds, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO growth in recovery regimes yields positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or the signal loses predictive accuracy outside the specified regime.
